analysis_summary: The proposed method produced 6 finding(s) from 6 analysis obligation(s).

findings.summary: Potential unmediated JTAG-to-AXI debug master: the visible RTL allows TAP-selected debug commands to reach AXI master issue logic without an observed authorization/lifecycle/range check and with security attributes hardwired to zero.
findings.vulnerability_category: Issue AXI master read/write transactions through axi_master_aw_valid, axi_master_ar_valid, axi_master_w_valid, response handling, and read-data return paths. / Reachable AXI address space exposed on adv_dbg_if/adbg_top/adbg_axi_module AXI master ports; exact attached SoC targets were not fully proven from the truncated integration facts. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External JTAG/TAP debug requester selecting the advanced debug AXI module. Operation: Issue AXI master read/write transactions through axi_master_aw_valid, axi_master_ar_valid, axi_master_w_valid, response handling, and read-data return paths. Object: Reachable AXI address space exposed on adv_dbg_if/adbg_top/adbg_axi_module AXI master ports; exact attached SoC targets were not fully proven from the truncated integration facts. Expected guard: A debug authorization or lifecycle/debug-enable gate before AXI requests are generated, plus range mediation and correct AXI security attributes. Observed behavior: The inspected RTL shows TAP debug selection and module selection gating, but no visible authorization/lifecycle input in the adv_dbg_if/adbg_top/adbg_axi_module/adbg_axi_biu path. adbg_top latches module_id_reg when debug_select_i, select_cmd, update_dr_i, and !select_inhibit are true, then selects AXI module 0 by default after reset. adbg_axi_module instantiates adbg_axi_biu directly. adbg_axi_biu forwards addr_reg directly to both axi_master_ar_addr and axi_master_aw_addr, drives write data/strb directly from latched debug data, and hardwires AXI PROT/USER/ID/region/cache/qos fields to zero. The search for auth in ips/adv_dbg_if/rtl found no relevant authorization signal beyond comments containing Author. Path: JTAG/TAP -> adv_dbg_if cluster_tap_i/adbg_tap_top debug_select_o -> adbg_top input_shift_reg/module_selects -> adbg_axi_module -> adbg_axi_biu -> axi_master_* ports. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If the AXI master is connected to protected memory or MMIO, an external JTAG actor that can select the debug instruction can read or write SoC resources without an RTL-visible authorization or range check, potentially bypassing CPU privilege, firmware locks, PMP-like policy, or boot/lifecycle restrictions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact top-level AXI attachment and protected target ranges were not fully available in the truncated fact layer. It remains possible that an external SoC-level interconnect or lifecycle wrapper gates this master outside the inspected snippets, but no such guard was observed in the provided evidence.

findings.summary: Potential unmediated JTAG-to-LINT memory/interconnect access: visible RTL maps JTAG debug commands to the LINT debug bus without observed authorization or range mediation.
findings.vulnerability_category: Generate LINT/TCDM requests using lint_req_o, lint_wen_o, lint_add_o, lint_wdata_o, lint_be_o and return read data through TDO path. / s_lint_debug_bus in pulp_soc, connected to the SoC interconnect lint_debug port; L2/TCDM/local interconnect targets are plausibly reachable through that bus, although the full address map was not provided. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG LINT debug interface through lint_jtag_wrap and adbg_lintonly_top/adbg_lint_module. Operation: Generate LINT/TCDM requests using lint_req_o, lint_wen_o, lint_add_o, lint_wdata_o, lint_be_o and return read data through TDO path. Object: s_lint_debug_bus in pulp_soc, connected to the SoC interconnect lint_debug port; L2/TCDM/local interconnect targets are plausibly reachable through that bus, although the full address map was not provided. Expected guard: Debug authorization/lifecycle/debug-enable gate and address-range mediation before asserting LINT requests to memory/interconnect. Observed behavior: pulp_soc instantiates lint_jtag_wrap i_lint_jtag, driven directly by JTAG TAP signals and jtag_axireg_sel_i, and connects its Master interface to s_lint_debug_bus. lint_jtag_wrap instantiates adbg_lintonly_top with debug_select_i tied to lint_select_i and maps lint_req/add/wen/wdata/be directly to jtag_lint_master. adbg_lintonly_top uses debug_select_i only for chain select and shifting; its module_id_reg resets to 0 and selects the bus module by default. adbg_lint_module instantiates adbg_lint_biu directly. adbg_lint_biu latches address/data/write/byte-enable on strobe and directly assigns lint_add_o, lint_wdata_o, lint_be_o; lint_aux_o is hardwired to zero. No authorization or range check was observed in the provided LINT debug path. Path: JTAG TAP signals in pulp_soc -> lint_jtag_wrap i_lint_jtag -> adbg_lintonly_top -> adbg_lint_module -> adbg_lint_biu -> s_lint_debug_bus -> i_soc_interconnect_wrap.lint_debug. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: If s_lint_debug_bus reaches L2/TCDM or memory-mapped control state, an external JTAG actor can issue local interconnect reads/writes without observed authorization or address restriction, potentially exposing secrets in memory or altering control state that normal software permissions would protect.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The full interconnect decode and list of protected LINT/TCDM/L2 targets were not included. The facts name both s_lint_jtag_bus and s_lint_debug_bus, but the observed wrapper connects to s_lint_debug_bus; no evidence showed downstream filtering for this debug master.

findings.summary: Potential unauthorized TDO disclosure path: debug read data/status can be shifted to external TDO under TAP/module selection without an observed debug authorization gate.
findings.vulnerability_category: Shift out module TDO, bus read data, CRC/status/internal register values, and potentially CPU debug status via selected debug modules. / debug_tdo_i/tdo_o path, adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module module_tdo_o, data_out_shift_reg/read-data path, internal error/status registers, and OR1K status/debug signals where instantiated. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: External JTAG/TAP scanner observing TDO. Operation: Shift out module TDO, bus read data, CRC/status/internal register values, and potentially CPU debug status via selected debug modules. Object: debug_tdo_i/tdo_o path, adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module module_tdo_o, data_out_shift_reg/read-data path, internal error/status registers, and OR1K status/debug signals where instantiated. Expected guard: TAP instruction selection plus debug authorization should gate shifting protected read data or internal status to TDO. Observed behavior: adbg_tap_top decodes the DEBUG instruction to assert debug_select and muxes tdo_mux_out to debug_tdo_i for DEBUG. adbg_top and adbg_lintonly_top shift input registers only when debug_select_i && shift_dr_i and choose module TDO based on module_id_reg. However, no authorization/lifecycle gate was observed. In adbg_lintonly_top, tdo_o is selected solely by module_id_reg between tdo_axi and tdo_cpu, with no debug_select_i in the final TDO mux. The AXI/LINT modules use CRC/data-out shift logic to serialize read data/status through module_tdo_o. Path: External TDO path: adbg_tap_top DEBUG instruction mux -> debug_tdo_i from adbg_top/adbg_lintonly_top -> selected module_tdo_o -> data/status shift registers from AXI/LINT/CPU debug modules. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: Protected bus read data or internal debug/CPU status can be serialized to the external TDO pin when the debug scan chain is selected. If debug should be disabled in certain lifecycle or locked states, absence of an observed authorization gate can leak memory contents, secret state, or control/status information.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The provided observations do not include a complete readout of all module_tdo_o assignments or OR1K status-register muxing. It is also uncertain whether a higher-level lifecycle controller disables the TAP externally. No such gate is visible in the provided RTL evidence.

findings.summary: adv_dbg_if exposes CPU debug ports including cpu_stall_o and cpu_rst_o. adbg_top includes CPU signals and module selection logic, but the detailed OR1K instantiation/wiring was not fully present in the observations. The file inventory confirms adbg_or1k_module, adbg_or1k_biu, and adbg_or1k_status_reg exist. Search observations show adbg_or1k_status_reg comments describing stall_bp holding stall until debug host acts and stall_reg_cpu allowing debug host to control a stall. However, the provided snippets do not prove whether the OR1K path is instantiated and connected in this PULPissimo integration, nor whether it is disconnected or externally gated.
findings.vulnerability_category: CPU stall/reset/debug register read/write and CPU status observation. / cpu_addr_o, cpu_data_o, cpu_stall_o, cpu_stb_o, cpu_we_o, cpu_rst_o, cpu_bp_i/cpu_ack_i/cpu_data_i paths in adv_dbg_if/adbg_top and OR1K module/status register files. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG OR1K/CPU debug module requester in the advanced debug RTL. Operation: CPU stall/reset/debug register read/write and CPU status observation. Object: cpu_addr_o, cpu_data_o, cpu_stall_o, cpu_stb_o, cpu_we_o, cpu_rst_o, cpu_bp_i/cpu_ack_i/cpu_data_i paths in adv_dbg_if/adbg_top and OR1K module/status register files. Expected guard: Explicit debug authorization and CPU debug-mode/privilege mediation before JTAG can halt, reset, or observe CPU debug/status state. Observed behavior: adv_dbg_if exposes CPU debug ports including cpu_stall_o and cpu_rst_o. adbg_top includes CPU signals and module selection logic, but the detailed OR1K instantiation/wiring was not fully present in the observations. The file inventory confirms adbg_or1k_module, adbg_or1k_biu, and adbg_or1k_status_reg exist. Search observations show adbg_or1k_status_reg comments describing stall_bp holding stall until debug host acts and stall_reg_cpu allowing debug host to control a stall. However, the provided snippets do not prove whether the OR1K path is instantiated and connected in this PULPissimo integration, nor whether it is disconnected or externally gated. Path: Potential path: JTAG/TAP -> adbg_top module select -> adbg_or1k_module/adbg_or1k_biu/status_reg -> CPU debug control outputs in adv_dbg_if/adbg_top -> CPU/domain integration. Complete reachability to actual CPU was not proven. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: If connected, an external JTAG actor could halt, stall, reset, or inspect CPU debug state without an observed authorization guard, affecting execution integrity and confidentiality of privileged CPU state. If unconnected in this SoC, this impact is not applicable.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The observations do not include complete OR1K module instantiation and top-level PULPissimo CPU wiring. It is uncertain whether this CPU debug path is used, tied off, or disconnected in the actual integration. No explicit authorization signal was observed in the available snippets, but absence cannot be concluded for the CPU path without complete wiring.

findings.summary: Several reset behaviors were observed: adbg_top resets module_id_reg and input_shift_reg on trstn_i; adbg_lintonly_top similarly resets module_id_reg; AXI BIU resets rdy_o, err_reg, data_out_reg, and synchronizer flops under trstn_i or axi_aresetn; LINT BIU resets sel_reg, addr_reg, data_in_reg, wr_reg, str_sync, ready synchronizers, err_reg, and data_out_reg under trstn_i/rstn_i. The command start path uses toggle-based synchronization from TCK to AXI/LINT clock domains. However, the evidence is incomplete for all FSM/request-output reset assignments and for interactions when only one of TCK reset and system reset toggles. No lifecycle authorization signal was observed to recheck after reset release.
findings.vulnerability_category: Retain, replay, or trigger pending debug bus operations across trstn_i, axi_aresetn, rstn_i/rst_ni, or mode transitions. / module_id_reg, input_shift_reg, address/data/sel/wr registers, strobe/ready synchronizers, data_out_reg, error state, and BIU FSM/request signals. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: Debug command path state across TCK, AXI/system clock, and LINT clock domains. Operation: Retain, replay, or trigger pending debug bus operations across trstn_i, axi_aresetn, rstn_i/rst_ni, or mode transitions. Object: module_id_reg, input_shift_reg, address/data/sel/wr registers, strobe/ready synchronizers, data_out_reg, error state, and BIU FSM/request signals. Expected guard: Reset and lifecycle/mode transitions should clear pending debug state and recheck authorization at the use site before bus outputs are asserted. Observed behavior: Several reset behaviors were observed: adbg_top resets module_id_reg and input_shift_reg on trstn_i; adbg_lintonly_top similarly resets module_id_reg; AXI BIU resets rdy_o, err_reg, data_out_reg, and synchronizer flops under trstn_i or axi_aresetn; LINT BIU resets sel_reg, addr_reg, data_in_reg, wr_reg, str_sync, ready synchronizers, err_reg, and data_out_reg under trstn_i/rstn_i. The command start path uses toggle-based synchronization from TCK to AXI/LINT clock domains. However, the evidence is incomplete for all FSM/request-output reset assignments and for interactions when only one of TCK reset and system reset toggles. No lifecycle authorization signal was observed to recheck after reset release. Path: TAP reset trstn_i and SoC resets axi_aresetn/rstn_i -> module selection and BIU state registers -> synchronized start_toggle -> AXI/LINT request outputs and read-data/TDO registers. Closure verdict: Related RTL evidence exists, but some core evidence slots remain incomplete.
findings.security_impact: A stale or partially reset debug command could cause an unexpected bus transaction after reset release, or expose stale read data on TDO. If debug authorization is supposed to be revoked by reset/lifecycle transition, absence of a recheck at the bus issue point could permit post-reset execution of previously shifted debug commands.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The provided observations are truncated before all AXI/LINT FSM output assignments and request-valid reset behavior. Reset alignment between trstn_i and axi_aresetn/rstn_i was not fully analyzed. No lifecycle/debug authorization state exists in the observed snippets, so reset reauthorization cannot be confirmed.

findings.summary: Potential arbitrary-address debug bus access: command-provided addresses, sizes, and byte enables are forwarded to AXI/LINT fabric without observed range, alignment, or permission mediation.
findings.vulnerability_category: Configure 32-bit address, select burst read/write opcodes, choose byte/halfword/word/doubleword sizes, and perform partial or full read/write via AXI or LINT BIU. / Protected MMIO, L2/TCDM/memory, and write-protected control words reachable by AXI or LINT debug masters. / permission-related RTL security issue
findings.reasoning_summary: Subject: JTAG/advanced debug bus command issuer controlling address, size, write data, and byte enables. Operation: Configure 32-bit address, select burst read/write opcodes, choose byte/halfword/word/doubleword sizes, and perform partial or full read/write via AXI or LINT BIU. Object: Protected MMIO, L2/TCDM/memory, and write-protected control words reachable by AXI or LINT debug masters. Expected guard: Address-range, alignment, size, byte-enable, and read/write permission checks before forwarding debug transactions to the fabric; invalid size encodings should error rather than default to a broad access. Observed behavior: AXI and LINT BIUs accept addr_i[31:0] from module address_counter and latch it into addr_reg on strobe. AXI BIU directly assigns addr_reg to axi_master_ar_addr and axi_master_aw_addr; LINT BIU directly assigns addr_reg to lint_add_o. Write strobes/byte enables are derived from size/address decode into sel_reg and then directly assigned to AXI w_strb or LINT be. No range comparison, protected-region table, privilege check, alignment rejection, or explicit permission denial was observed. AXI word_size_i case defaults to 64-bit AXI size encoding for invalid values. adbg_axi_defines exposes opcodes for BWRITE8/16/32/64 and BREAD8/16/32/64. The LINT path similarly forwards latched address/data/byte-enable to the local bus. Path: JTAG command/data_register_i -> adbg_axi_module/adbg_lint_module operation/address counters -> biu_strobe -> adbg_axi_biu/adbg_lint_biu addr_reg/sel_reg/data_in_reg -> AXI/LINT fabric outputs. Closure verdict: All required core evidence slots are present for a candidate violation.
findings.security_impact: An external debug actor can potentially target arbitrary fabric addresses and use partial writes to modify protected control words or read protected memory/MMIO. Invalid or unaligned size/address combinations may be forwarded rather than blocked, enabling bypass of intended software-level permissions or register write-protection assumptions.
findings.uncertainty_or_missing_evidence: The exact protected ranges and downstream interconnect access-control behavior are not fully provided. It is uncertain whether external wrappers or interconnect filters restrict these debug masters after the BIU outputs. No such filtering was present in the observed debug module RTL.

global_uncertainty: No closure-level missing evidence was recorded beyond the visible source scope limitations.
